scope: Independently authored graded relevance judgments over the companion corpus.
provenance: Authored by the repository owner against the profile list alone, without reading config/concepts.json. That independence is checkable rather than asserted: the concept vocabulary fires on none of these queries, so none of them were written to match it.
protocol: Tune only on dev. Acceptance and abstention are frozen. Nothing in this repository was tuned against any group - dev was measured perfect on first run, so the acceptance figures are a clean held-out result.
interpretation_rule: Every query means 'who can help me with this'. Queries phrased as a situation rather than a person - acc_05 and acc_06 share the '[capability] without [thing]' shape - are person-queries, because this is a people search and staff type fragments. The rule is stated because acc_06 was challenged on exactly this point.
known_limitations: The five abstention queries are inherited from data/golden_queries.json and are unrealistically distant from the domain (Roman history, basketball). They score 0.30-0.43 against a 0.47 threshold, so passing them is weak evidence. Realistic near-misses - a divorce lawyer, a personal trainer - would be a harder test.

Fifteen of eighteen positive queries share vocabulary with their primary target, so this set largely measures retrieval rather than the zero-overlap semantic bridging the brief names. Only dev_04, acc_06 and acc_10 are genuinely zero-overlap.
The dev group contains no negatives, so the confidence gate cannot be recalibrated on dev without touching frozen groups.
